title: euphorbia littoralis
intro: Euphorbia littoralis, also known as the fire bush or spurge, is a succulent plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bright red or yellow flowers and spiny stems. Traditionally, Euphorbia littoralis has been used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its sap, latex, and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Euphorbia littoralis is often prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential skin irritation and other side effects.
